name: CrossSectionalMeanReversion_5D_USEquities
universe_definition: Median daily dollar volume > $5M over past 60 trading days
Exclude ADRs, ETFs, closed-end funds, SPACs, warrants, preferreds
Exclude stocks with less than 252 trading days of history
Top 1000 names by liquidity after filters
data_requirements: Shares outstanding or free-float market cap
signal_definition: Compute 5-day residual return versus sector-neutral cross-sectional model
Rank stocks cross-sectionally each rebalance date
Go long bottom decile of ranked residual returns
Go short top decile of ranked residual returns
Size positions by inverse volatility and normalized signal strength
Neutralize beta and sector exposures at portfolio construction
rebalancing_rule: Exit each tranche after 5 trading days
Skip names failing liquidity or price filters on entry date
transaction_cost_model: Slippage: max(5 bps, 0.1 * daily volatility * participation rate)
Borrow cost on shorts: 25 bps annualized baseline
Market impact scaled by ADV participation
Apply one-day execution delay in robustness tests
risk_model: Estimate daily volatility using 60-day exponential window
Constrain portfolio beta to market near zero
Constrain sector exposures to near zero
Single-name weight capped by liquidity and volatility
Monitor gross, net, factor, and idiosyncratic risk
constraints: Max daily traded notional per name <= 10% of ADV
No position in halted or missing-data names
validation_plan: Evaluate with and without transaction costs
Run subperiod tests by regime and volatility state
Check sensitivity to bucket size, holding period, and delay
failure_modes: Performance concentrated in few periods or sectors
Hidden exposure to size, value, or short-term reversal crowding
Short borrow unavailability or elevated borrow fees
Backtest bias from survivorship, lookahead, or stale fundamentals
minimum_acceptance_criteria: Average daily turnover <= 35% of gross exposure
Positive returns in at least 60% of rolling 12-month windows
Stable performance across top-level sectors and market regimes
No single year contributes more than 35% of total PnL
